NKJV.1: Stand fast therefore in the liberty by which Christ has made us free, and do not be entangled again with a yoke of bondage.
NKJV.2: Indeed I, Paul, say to you that if you become circumcised, Christ will profit you nothing.
NKJV.3: And I testify again to every man who becomes circumcised that he is a debtor to keep the whole law.
NKJV.4: You have become estranged from Christ, you who attempt to be justified by law; you have fallen from grace.
NKJV.5: For we through the Spirit eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.
NKJV.6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision avails anything, but faith working through love.
NKJV.7: You ran well. Who hindered you from obeying the truth?
NKJV.8: This persuasion does not come from Him who calls you.
NKJV.9: A little leaven leavens the whole lump.
NKJV.10: I have confidence in you, in the Lord, that you will have no other mind; but he who troubles you shall bear his judgment, whoever he is.
NKJV.11: And I, brethren, if I still preach circumcision, why do I still suffer persecution? Then the offense of the cross has ceased.
NKJV.12: I could wish that those who trouble you would even cut themselves off!
NKJV.13: For you, brethren, have been called to liberty; only do not use liberty as an opportunity for the flesh, but through love serve one another.
NKJV.14: For all the law is fulfilled in one word, even in this: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
NKJV.15: But if you bite and devour one another, beware lest you be consumed by one another!
NKJV.16: I say then: Walk in the Spirit, and you shall not fulfill the lust of the flesh.
NKJV.17: For the flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; and these are contrary to one another, so that you do not do the things that you wish.
NKJV.18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
NKJV.19: Now the works of the flesh are evident, which are: adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lewdness,
NKJV.20: idolatry, sorcery, hatred, contentions, jealousies, outbursts of wrath, selfish ambitions, dissensions, heresies,
NKJV.21: envy, murders, drunkenness, revelries, and the like; of which I tell you beforehand, just as I also told you in time past, that those who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
NKJV.22: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
NKJV.23: gentleness, self-control. Against such there is no law.
NKJV.24: And those who are Christ’s have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
NKJV.25: If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.
NKJV.26: Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, envying one another.

ESV.1: For freedom Christ has set us free; stand firm therefore, and do not submit again to a yoke of slavery.
ESV.2: Look: I, Paul, say to you that if you accept circumcision, Christ will be of no advantage to you.
ESV.3: I testify again to every man who accepts circumcision that he is obligated to keep the whole law.
ESV.4: You are severed from Christ, you who would be justified  by the law; you have fallen away from grace.
ESV.5: For through the Spirit, by faith, we ourselves eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness.
ESV.6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision counts for anything, but only faith working through love.
ESV.7: You were running well. Who hindered you from obeying the truth?
ESV.8: This persuasion is not from him who calls you.
ESV.9: A little leaven leavens the whole lump.
ESV.10: I have confidence in the Lord that you will take no other view, and the one who is troubling you will bear the penalty, whoever he is.
ESV.11: But if I, brothers,  still preach  circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? In that case the offense of the cross has been removed.
ESV.12: I wish those who unsettle you would emasculate themselves!
ESV.13: For you were called to freedom, brothers. Only do not use your freedom as an opportunity for the flesh, but through love serve one another.
ESV.14: For the whole law is fulfilled in one word: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
ESV.15: But if you bite and devour one another, watch out that you are not consumed by one another.
ESV.16: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.
ESV.17: For the desires of the flesh are against the Spirit, and the desires of the Spirit are against the flesh, for these are opposed to each other, to keep you from doing the things you want to do.
ESV.18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
ESV.19: Now the works of the flesh are evident: sexual immorality, impurity, sensuality,
ESV.20: idolatry, sorcery, enmity, strife, jealousy, fits of anger, rivalries, dissensions, divisions,
ESV.21: envy,  drunkenness, orgies, and things like these. I warn you, as I warned you before, that those who do  such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
ESV.22: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
ESV.23: gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no law.
ESV.24: And those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
ESV.25: If we live by the Spirit, let us also keep in step with the Spirit.
ESV.26: Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, envying one another.

NASB.1: It was for freedom that Christ set us free; therefore keep standing firm and do not be subject again to a yoke of slavery.
NASB.2: Behold I, Paul, say to you that if you receive circumcision, Christ will be of no benefit to you.
NASB.3: And I testify again to every man who receives circumcision, that he is under obligation to keep the whole Law.
NASB.4: You have been severed from Christ, you who are seeking to be justified by law; you have fallen from grace.
NASB.5: For we through the Spirit, by faith, are waiting for the hope of righteousness.
NASB.6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision means anything, but faith working through love.
NASB.7: You were running well; who hindered you from obeying the truth?
NASB.8: This persuasion [did] not [come] from Him who calls you.
NASB.9: A little leaven leavens the whole lump [of dough].
NASB.10: I have confidence in you in the Lord that you will adopt no other view; but the one who is disturbing you will bear his judgment, whoever he is.
NASB.11: But I, brethren, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still persecuted? Then the stumbling block of the cross has been abolished.
NASB.12: I wish that those who are troubling you would even mutilate themselves.
NASB.13: For you were called to freedom, brethren; only [do] not [turn] your freedom into an opportunity for the flesh, but through love serve one another.
NASB.14: For the whole Law is fulfilled in one word, in the [statement], 'YOU SHALL LOVE YOUR NEIGHBOR AS YOURSELF.'
NASB.15: But if you bite and devour one another, take care that you are not consumed by one another.
NASB.16: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not carry out the desire of the flesh.
NASB.17: For the flesh sets its desire against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; for these are in opposition to one another, so that you may not do the things that you please.
NASB.18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the Law.
NASB.19: Now the deeds of the flesh are evident, which are: immorality, impurity, sensuality,
NASB.20: idolatry, sorcery, enmities, strife, jealousy, outbursts of anger, disputes, dissensions, factions,
NASB.21: envying, drunkenness, carousing, and things like these, of which I forewarn you, just as I have forewarned you, that those who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
NASB.22: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
NASB.23: gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no law.
NASB.24: Now those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
NASB.25: If we live by the Spirit, let us also walk by the Spirit.
NASB.26: Let us not become boastful, challenging one another, envying one another.

NLT.1: So Christ has truly set us free. Now make sure that you stay free, and don’t get tied up again in slavery to the law.
NLT.2: Listen! I, Paul, tell you this: If you are counting on circumcision to make you right with God, then Christ will be of no benefit to you.
NLT.3: I’ll say it again. If you are trying to find favor with God by being circumcised, you must obey every regulation in the whole law of Moses.
NLT.4: For if you are trying to make yourselves right with God by keeping the law, you have been cut off from Christ! You have fallen away from God’s grace.
NLT.5: But we who live by the Spirit eagerly wait to receive by faith the righteousness God has promised to us.
NLT.6: For when we place our faith in Christ Jesus, there is no benefit in being circumcised or being uncircumcised. What is important is faith expressing itself in love.
NLT.7: You were running the race so well. Who has held you back from following the truth?
NLT.8: It certainly isn’t God, for he is the one who called you to freedom.
NLT.9: This false teaching is like a little yeast that spreads through the whole batch of dough!
NLT.10: I am trusting the Lord to keep you from believing false teachings. God will judge that person, whoever he is, who has been confusing you.
NLT.11: Dear brothers and sisters, if I were still preaching that you must be circumcised — as some say I do — why am I still being persecuted? If I were no longer preaching salvation through the cross of Christ, no one would be offended.
NLT.12: I just wish that those troublemakers who want to mutilate you by circumcision would mutilate themselves.
NLT.13: For you have been called to live in freedom, my brothers and sisters. But don’t use your freedom to satisfy your sinful nature. Instead, use your freedom to serve one another in love.
NLT.14: For the whole law can be summed up in this one command: “Love your neighbor as yourself.”
NLT.15: But if you are always biting and devouring one another, watch out! Beware of destroying one another.
NLT.16: So I say, let the Holy Spirit guide your lives. Then you won’t be doing what your sinful nature craves.
NLT.17: The sinful nature wants to do evil, which is just the opposite of what the Spirit wants. And the Spirit gives us desires that are the opposite of what the sinful nature desires. These two forces are constantly fighting each other, so you are not free to carry out your good intentions.
NLT.18: But when you are directed by the Spirit, you are not under obligation to the law of Moses.
NLT.19: When you follow the desires of your sinful nature, the results are very clear: sexual immorality, impurity, lustful pleasures,
NLT.20: idolatry, sorcery, hostility, quarreling, jealousy, outbursts of anger, selfish ambition, dissension, division,
NLT.21: envy, drunkenness, wild parties, and other sins like these. Let me tell you again, as I have before, that anyone living that sort of life will not inherit the Kingdom of God.
NLT.22: But the Holy Spirit produces this kind of fruit in our lives: love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
NLT.23: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against these things!
NLT.24: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have nailed the passions and desires of their sinful nature to his cross and crucified them there.
NLT.25: Since we are living by the Spirit, let us follow the Spirit’s leading in every part of our lives.
NLT.26: Let us not become conceited, or provoke one another, or be jealous of one another.

WEB.1: Stand firm therefore in the liberty by which Christ has made us free, and don’t be entangled again with a yoke of bondage.
WEB.2: Behold, I, Paul, tell you that if you receive circumcision, Christ will profit you nothing.
WEB.3: Yes, I testify again to every man who receives circumcision, that he is a debtor to do the whole law.
WEB.4: You are alienated from Christ, you who desire to be justified by the law. You have fallen away from grace.
WEB.5: For we, through the Spirit, by faith wait for the hope of righteousness.
WEB.6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision amounts to anything, nor uncircumcision, but faith working through love.
WEB.7: You were running well! Who interfered with you that you should not obey the truth?
WEB.8: This persuasion is not from him who calls you.
WEB.9: A little yeast grows through the whole lump.
WEB.10: I have confidence toward you in the Lord that you will think no other way. But he who troubles you will bear his judgment, whoever he is.
WEB.11: But I, brothers, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still persecuted? Then the stumbling block of the cross has been removed.
WEB.12: I wish that those who disturb you would cut themselves off.
WEB.13: For you, brothers, were called for freedom. Only don’t use your freedom for gain to the flesh, but through love be servants to one another.
WEB.14: For the whole law is fulfilled in one word, in this: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
WEB.15: But if you bite and devour one another, be careful that you don’t consume one another.
WEB.16: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you won’t fulfill the lust of the flesh.
WEB.17: For the flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; and these are contrary to one another, that you may not do the things that you desire.
WEB.18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
WEB.19: Now the deeds of the flesh are obvious, which are: adultery, sexual immorality, uncleanness, lustfulness,
WEB.20: idolatry, sorcery, hatred, strife, jealousies, outbursts of anger, rivalries, divisions, heresies,
WEB.21: envy, murders, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these; of which I forewarn you, even as I also forewarned you, that those who practice such things will not inherit God’s Kingdom.
WEB.22: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith,
WEB.23: gentleness, and self-control. Against such things there is no law.
WEB.24: Those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh with its passions and lusts.
WEB.25: If we live by the Spirit, let’s also walk by the Spirit.
WEB.26: Let’s not become conceited, provoking one another, and envying one another.

CSB17.1: For freedom, Christ set us free. Stand firm <sup>ⓛ</sup> then and don’t submit again to a yoke of slavery. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.2: Take note! I, Paul, am telling you that if you get yourselves circumcised, Christ will not benefit you at all.
CSB17.3: Again I testify to every man who gets himself circumcised that he is obligated to do the entire law.
CSB17.4: You who are trying to be justified by the law are alienated from Christ; you have fallen from grace. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.5: For we eagerly await <sup>ⓐ</sup> through the Spirit, by faith, the hope <sup>ⓑ</sup> of righteousness.
CSB17.6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision accomplishes anything; what matters is faith <sup>ⓒ</sup> working through love. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.7: You were running well. Who prevented you from being persuaded regarding the truth? <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.8: This persuasion does not come from the one who calls you. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.9: A little leaven <sup>[2]</sup> leavens the whole batch of dough.
CSB17.10: I myself am persuaded in the Lord you will not accept any other view. But whoever it is that is confusing you will pay the penalty. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.11: Now brothers and sisters, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still persecuted? In that case the offense of the cross <sup>ⓗ</sup> has been abolished.
CSB17.12: I wish those who are disturbing you might also let themselves be mutilated!
CSB17.13: For you were called <sup>ⓘ</sup> to be free, brothers and sisters; only don’t use this freedom as an opportunity <sup>[3]</sup> for the flesh, but serve one another through love.
CSB17.14: For the whole law is fulfilled ⓙ in one statement: Love your neighbor as yourself. [4] ⓚ
CSB17.15: But if you bite and devour <sup>ⓛ</sup> one another, watch out, or you will be consumed by one another.
CSB17.16: I say then, walk by the Spirit <sup>ⓜ</sup> and you will certainly not carry out the desire of the flesh.
CSB17.17: For the flesh desires <sup>ⓝ</sup> what is against the Spirit, and the Spirit desires what is against the flesh; these are opposed to each other, so that you don’t do what you want. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
CSB17.19: Now the works of the flesh are obvious: <sup>[5]</sup> sexual immorality, <sup>ⓑ</sup> moral impurity, <sup>ⓒ</sup> promiscuity, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.20: idolatry, <sup>ⓔ</sup> sorcery, <sup>ⓕ</sup> hatreds, <sup>ⓖ</sup> strife, <sup>ⓗ</sup> jealousy, <sup>ⓘ</sup> outbursts of anger, <sup>ⓙ</sup> selfish ambitions, <sup>ⓚ</sup> dissensions, <sup>ⓛ</sup> factions, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.21: envy, <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> drunkenness, carousing, <sup>ⓐ</sup> and anything similar. I am warning you about these things — as I warned you before — that those who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.22: But the fruit of the Spirit <sup>ⓒ</sup> is love, <sup>ⓓ</sup> joy, <sup>ⓔ</sup> peace, patience, <sup>ⓕ</sup> kindness, <sup>ⓖ</sup> goodness, faithfulness,
CSB17.23: gentleness, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and self-control. <sup>ⓘ</sup> The law is not against such things. <sup>[7]</sup>
CSB17.24: Now those who belong to Christ Jesus <sup>ⓙ</sup> have crucified the flesh <sup>ⓚ</sup> with its passions and desires. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.25: If we live <sup>ⓜ</sup> by the Spirit, let us also keep in step with the Spirit.
CSB17.26: Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, envying one another. <sup>ⓝ</sup>

AMP.1: It was for this freedom that Christ set us free [completely liberating us]; therefore keep standing firm and do not be subject again to a yoke of slavery [which you once removed].
AMP.2: Notice, it is I, Paul, who tells you that if you receive circumcision [as a supposed requirement of salvation], Christ will be of no benefit to you [for you will lack the faith in Christ that is necessary for salvation].
AMP.3: Once more I solemnly affirm to every man who receives circumcision [as a supposed requirement of salvation], that he is under obligation and required to keep the whole Law.
AMP.4: You have been severed from Christ, if you seek to be justified [that is, declared free of the guilt of sin and its penalty, and placed in right standing with God] through the Law; you have fallen from grace [for you have lost your grasp on God's unmerited favor and blessing].
AMP.5: For we [not relying on the Law but] through the [strength and power of the Holy] Spirit, by faith, are waiting [confidently] for the hope of righteousness [the completion of our salvation].
AMP.6: For [if we are] in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision means anything, but only faith activated and expressed and working through love.
AMP.7: You were running [the race] well; who has interfered and prevented you from obeying the truth?
AMP.8: This [deceptive] persuasion is not from Him who called you [to freedom in Christ].
AMP.9: A little leaven [a slight inclination to error, or a few false teachers] leavens the whole batch [it perverts the concept of faith and misleads the church].
AMP.10: I have confidence in you in the Lord that you will adopt no other view [contrary to mine on the matter]; but the one who is disturbing you, whoever he is, will have to bear the penalty.
AMP.11: But as for me, brothers, if I am still preaching circumcision [as I had done before I met Christ; and as some accuse me of doing now, as necessary for salvation], why am I still being persecuted [by Jews]? In that case the stumbling block of the cross [to unbelieving Jews] has been abolished.
AMP.12: I wish that those who are troubling you [by teaching that circumcision is necessary for salvation] would even [go all the way and] castrate themselves!
AMP.13: For you, my brothers, were called to freedom; only do not let your freedom become an opportunity for the sinful nature (worldliness, selfishness), but through love serve and seek the best for one another.
AMP.14: For the whole Law [concerning human relationships] is fulfilled in one precept, "You shall love your neighbor as yourself [that is, you shall have an unselfish concern for others and do things for their benefit]."
AMP.15: But if you bite and devour one another [in bickering and strife], watch out that you [along with your entire fellowship] are not consumed by one another.
AMP.16: But I say, walk habitually in the [Holy] Spirit [seek Him and be responsive to His guidance], and then you will certainly not carry out the desire of the sinful nature [which responds impulsively without regard for God and His precepts].
AMP.17: For the sinful nature has its desire which is opposed to the Spirit, and the [desire of the] Spirit opposes the sinful nature; for these [two, the sinful nature and the Spirit] are in direct opposition to each other [continually in conflict], so that you [as believers] do not [always] do whatever [good things] you want to do.
AMP.18: But if you are guided and led by the Spirit, you are not subject to the Law.
AMP.19: Now the practices of the sinful nature are clearly evident: they are sexual immorality, impurity, sensuality (total irresponsibility, lack of self-control),
AMP.20: idolatry, sorcery, hostility, strife, jealousy, fits of anger, disputes, dissensions, factions [that promote heresies],
AMP.21: envy, drunkenness, riotous behavior, and other things like these. I warn you beforehand, just as I did previously, that those who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
AMP.22: But the fruit of the Spirit [the result of His presence within us] is love [unselfish concern for others], joy, [inner] peace, patience [not the ability to wait, but how we act while waiting], kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
AMP.23: gentleness, self-control. Against such things there is no law.
AMP.24: And those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the sinful nature together with its passions and appetites.
AMP.25: If we [claim to] live by the [Holy] Spirit, we must also walk by the Spirit [with personal integrity, godly character, and moral courage—our conduct empowered by the Holy Spirit].
AMP.26: We must not become conceited, challenging or provoking one another, envying one another.

MSG.1: Christ has set us free to live a free life. So take your stand! Never again let anyone put a harness of slavery on you.
MSG.2: I am emphatic about this. The moment any one of you submits to circumcision or any other rule-keeping system, at that same moment Christ's hard-won gift of freedom is squandered.
MSG.3: I repeat my warning: The person who accepts the ways of circumcision trades all the advantages of the free life in Christ for the obligations of the slave life of the law.
MSG.4: I suspect you would never intend this, but this is what happens. When you attempt to live by your own religious plans and projects, you are cut off from Christ, you fall out of grace.
MSG.5: Meanwhile we expectantly wait for a satisfying relationship with the Spirit.
MSG.6: For in Christ, neither our most conscientious religion nor disregard of religion amounts to anything. What matters is something far more interior: faith expressed in love.
MSG.7: You were running superbly! Who cut in on you, deflecting you from the true course of obedience?
MSG.8: This detour doesn't come from the One who called you into the race in the first place.
MSG.9: And please don't toss this off as insignificant. It only takes a minute amount of yeast, you know, to permeate an entire loaf of bread.
MSG.10: Deep down, the Master has given me confidence that you will not defect. But the one who is upsetting you, whoever he is, will bear the divine judgment.
MSG.11: As for the rumor that I continue to preach the ways of circumcision (as I did in those pre-Damascus Road days), that is absurd. Why would I still be persecuted, then? If I were preaching that old message, no one would be offended if I mentioned the Cross now and then—it would be so watered-down it wouldn't matter one way or the other.
MSG.12: Why don't these agitators, obsessive as they are about circumcision, go all the way and castrate themselves!
MSG.13: It is absolutely clear that God has called you to a free life. Just make sure that you don't use this freedom as an excuse to do whatever you want to do and destroy your freedom. Rather, use your freedom to serve one another in love; that's how freedom grows.
MSG.14: For everything we know about God's Word is summed up in a single sentence: Love others as you love yourself. That's an act of true freedom.
MSG.15: If you bite and ravage each other, watch out—in no time at all you will be annihilating each other, and where will your precious freedom be then?
MSG.16: My counsel is this: Live freely, animated and motivated by God's Spirit. Then you won't feed the compulsions of selfishness.
MSG.17: For there is a root of sinful self-interest in us that is at odds with a free spirit, just as the free spirit is incompatible with selfishness. These two ways of life are antithetical, so that you cannot live at times one way and at times another way according to how you feel on any given day.
MSG.18: Why don't you choose to be led by the Spirit and so escape the erratic compulsions of a law-dominated existence?
MSG.19: It is obvious what kind of life develops out of trying to get your own way all the time: repetitive, loveless, cheap sex; a stinking accumulation of mental and emotional garbage; frenzied and joyless grabs for happiness;
MSG.20: trinket gods; magic-show religion; paranoid loneliness; cutthroat competition; all-consuming-yet-never-satisfied wants; a brutal temper; an impotence to love or be loved; divided homes and divided lives; small-minded and lopsided pursuits;
MSG.21: the vicious habit of depersonalizing everyone into a rival; uncontrolled and uncontrollable addictions; ugly parodies of community. I could go on. This isn't the first time I have warned you, you know. If you use your freedom this way, you will not inherit God's kingdom.
MSG.22: But what happens when we live God's way? He brings gifts into our lives, much the same way that fruit appears in an orchard—things like affection for others, exuberance about life, serenity. We develop a willingness to stick with things, a sense of compassion in the heart, and a conviction that a basic holiness permeates things and people. We find ourselves involved in loyal commitments,
MSG.23: not needing to force our way in life, able to marshal and direct our energies wisely. Legalism is helpless in bringing this about; it only gets in the way.
MSG.24: Among those who belong to Christ, everything connected with getting our own way and mindlessly responding to what everyone else calls necessities is killed off for good—crucified.
MSG.25: Since this is the kind of life we have chosen, the life of the Spirit, let us make sure that we do not just hold it as an idea in our heads or a sentiment in our hearts, but work out its implications in every detail of our lives.
MSG.26: That means we will not compare ourselves with each other as if one of us were better and another worse. We have far more interesting things to do with our lives. Each of us is an original.

NIV.1:
Freedom in Christ
It is for freedom that Christ has set us free. Stand firm, then, and do not let yourselves be burdened again by a yoke of slavery.
NIV.2: Mark my words! I, Paul, tell you that if you let yourselves be circumcised, Christ will be of no value to you at all.
NIV.3: Again I declare to every man who lets himself be circumcised that he is obligated to obey the whole law.
NIV.4: You who are trying to be justified by law have been alienated from Christ; you have fallen away from grace.
NIV.5: But by faith we eagerly await through the Spirit the righteousness for which we hope.
NIV.6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision has any value. The only thing that counts is faith expressing itself through love.
NIV.7: You were running a good race. Who cut in on you and kept you from obeying the truth?
NIV.8: That kind of persuasion does not come from the one who calls you.
NIV.9: “A little yeast works through the whole batch of dough.”
NIV.10: I am confident in the Lord that you will take no other view. The one who is throwing you into confusion will pay the penalty, whoever he may be.
NIV.11: Brothers, if I am still preaching circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? In that case the offense of the cross has been abolished.
NIV.12: As for those agitators, I wish they would go the whole way and emasculate themselves!
NIV.13: You, my brothers, were called to be free. But do not use your freedom to indulge the sinful nature; rather, serve one another in love.
NIV.14: The entire law is summed up in a single command: “Love your neighbor as yourself.”
NIV.15: If you keep on biting and devouring each other, watch out or you will be destroyed by each other.
NIV.16:
Life by the Spirit
So I say, live by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the sinful nature.
NIV.17: For the sinful nature desires what is contrary to the Spirit, and the Spirit what is contrary to the sinful nature. They are in conflict with each other, so that you do not do what you want.
NIV.18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under law.
NIV.19: The acts of the sinful nature are obvious: sexual immorality, impurity and debauchery;
NIV.20: idolatry and witchcraft; hatred, discord, jealousy, fits of rage, selfish ambition, dissensions, factions
NIV.21: and envy; drunkenness, orgies, and the like. I warn you, as I did before, that those who live like this will not inherit the kingdom of God.
NIV.22: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
NIV.23: gentleness and self-control. Against such things there is no law.
NIV.24: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the sinful nature with its passions and desires.
NIV.25: Since we live by the Spirit, let us keep in step with the Spirit.
NIV.26: Let us not become conceited, provoking and envying each other.

NRSVCE.1: For freedom Christ has set us free. Stand firm, therefore, and do not submit again to a yoke of slavery.
NRSVCE.2: Listen! I, Paul, am telling you that if you let yourselves be circumcised, Christ will be of no benefit to you.
NRSVCE.3: Once again I testify to every man who lets himself be circumcised that he is obliged to obey the entire law.
NRSVCE.4: You who want to be justified by the law have cut yourselves off from Christ; you have fallen away from grace.
NRSVCE.5: For through the Spirit, by faith, we eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness.
NRSVCE.6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision counts for anything; the only thing that counts is faith working through love.
NRSVCE.7: You were running well; who prevented you from obeying the truth?
NRSVCE.8: Such persuasion does not come from the one who calls you.
NRSVCE.9: A little yeast leavens the whole batch of dough.
NRSVCE.10: I am confident about you in the Lord that you will not think otherwise. But whoever it is that is confusing you will pay the penalty.
NRSVCE.11: But my friends, why am I still being persecuted if I am still preaching circumcision? In that case the offense of the cross has been removed.
NRSVCE.12: I wish those who unsettle you would castrate themselves!
NRSVCE.13: For you were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only do not use your freedom as an opportunity for self-indulgence, but through love become slaves to one another.
NRSVCE.14: For the whole law is summed up in a single commandment, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
NRSVCE.15: If, however, you bite and devour one another, take care that you are not consumed by one another.
NRSVCE.16: Live by the Spirit, I say, and do not gratify the desires of the flesh.
NRSVCE.17: For what the flesh desires is opposed to the Spirit, and what the Spirit desires is opposed to the flesh; for these are opposed to each other, to prevent you from doing what you want.
NRSVCE.18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not subject to the law.
NRSVCE.19: Now the works of the flesh are obvious: fornication, impurity, licentiousness,
NRSVCE.20: idolatry, sorcery, enmities, strife, jealousy, anger, quarrels, dissensions, factions,
NRSVCE.21: envy, drunkenness, carousing, and things like these. I am warning you, as I warned you before: those who do such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
NRSVCE.22: By contrast, the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, generosity, faithfulness,
NRSVCE.23: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against such things.
NRSVCE.24: And those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
NRSVCE.25: If we live by the Spirit, let us also be guided by the Spirit.
NRSVCE.26: Let us not become conceited, competing against one another, envying one another.

NET.1: For freedom Christ has set us free. Stand firm, then, and do not be subject again to the yoke of slavery.
NET.2: Listen! I, Paul, tell you that if you let yourselves be circumcised, Christ will be of no benefit to you at all!
NET.3: And I testify again to every man who lets himself be circumcised that he is obligated to obey the whole law.
NET.4: You who are trying to be declared righteous by the law have been alienated from Christ; you have fallen away from grace!
NET.5: For through the Spirit, by faith, we wait expectantly for the hope of righteousness.
NET.6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision carries any weight — the only thing that matters is faith working through love.
NET.7: You were running well; who prevented you from obeying the truth?
NET.8: This persuasion does not come from the one who calls you!
NET.9: A little yeast makes the whole batch of dough rise!
NET.10: I am confident in the Lord that you will accept no other view. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever he may be.
NET.11: Now, brothers and sisters, if I am still preaching circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? In that case the offense of the cross has been removed.
NET.12: I wish those agitators would go so far as to castrate themselves!
NET.13: For you were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only do not use your freedom as an opportunity to indulge your flesh, but through love serve one another.
NET.14: For the whole law can be summed up in a single commandment, namely, “You must love your neighbor as yourself.”
NET.15: However, if you continually bite and devour one another, beware that you are not consumed by one another.
NET.16: But I say, live by the Spirit and you will not carry out the desires of the flesh.
NET.17: For the flesh has desires that are opposed to the Spirit, and the Spirit has desires that are opposed to the flesh, for these are in opposition to each other, so that you cannot do what you want.
NET.18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
NET.19: Now the works of the flesh are obvious: sexual immorality, impurity, depravity,
NET.20: idolatry, sorcery, hostilities, strife, jealousy, outbursts of anger, selfish rivalries, dissensions, factions,
NET.21: envying, murder, drunkenness, carousing, and similar things. I am warning you, as I had warned you before: Those who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God!
NET.22: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
NET.23: gentleness, and self-control. Against such things there is no law.
NET.24: Now those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
NET.25: If we live by the Spirit, let us also behave in accordance with the Spirit.
NET.26: Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, being jealous of one another.
